2. Online Assessment: Typically conducted on platforms which is designed to evaluate your technical and problem-solving abilities. This round generally includes questions on Python programming, SQL queries, statistics fundamentals, data manipulation, and basic machine learning concepts. Candidates may be asked to solve coding problems, write optimized SQL queries, clean and analyze datasets, or answer conceptual questions related to machine learning and analytics.
[...]
3. Technical Interview: It usually consist of one to three interviews that assess your depth of knowledge in machine learning, statistics, and programming. Common topics include machine learning fundamentals such as bias vs variance, overfitting and underfitting, comparisons like Random Forest vs XGBoost, and evaluation metrics such as precision and recall. Along with these concepts, candidates are often asked about feature engineering, model selection, and practical implementation scenarios to test both theoretical understanding and real-world application skills.
